data.description:
# REVIEW_RESULT reducer-ignored sidecar event

## Why
We need cheap, durable capture of every review for offline FP/remediation analysis (the enabler for follow-ons F1-F4), without taxing rebar's hot paths the way a large state-bearing event would.

## What
A new reducer-IGNORED sidecar event type carrying the bulky review observability, co-located on the ticket's event stream but invisible to head/.cache/deps/validate.

## Scope
- REVIEW_RESULT event: per-criterion verdict + kind + exec, finding fingerprint (hash of criterion+normalized location+text), short finding text + citation, model self-reported confidence, chunking config, budget cap/spent, signature-minted, prior review_id.
- The reducer intentionally SKIPS this event type (deliberate preserved-and-ignored), so it never folds into head/.cache and is unseen by deps/validate/health (the session_log out-of-hot-paths treatment at the event-type level).
- Compactable/archivable after offline extraction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A REVIEW_RESULT event type with the fields above; emitted once per review.
- [ ] The reducer skips the type — a test asserts head state / .cache / deps / validate are unaffected by REVIEW_RESULT events.
- [ ] An offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints + existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) reconstructs per-criterion fire/remediation/FP rates from the events alone.
data.title: REVIEW_RESULT reducer-ignored sidecar event
